name: Explicit Boundaries
summary: Keep transport, application, domain, and infrastructure responsibilities explicit so changes stay localized.

rules: Name the boundary crossed by each adapter and keep mapping code at that boundary.
Do not let transport concerns leak into use-case or domain code.
constraints: Framework extractors, response types, and HTTP status codes stay outside the use-case boundary.
checks: The endpoint can be changed without modifying core use-case logic unless behavior changed.

blocks.markdown: Explicit boundaries make responsibilities inspectable. Handlers adapt protocols, use cases coordinate behavior, domain code enforces invariants, and infrastructure performs external I/O.

items: Keep request extraction and response construction in the transport layer.
Pass use-case input as application-owned data, not framework types.
Translate errors once at the outer boundary.
